outcome_contract.why: The owner dashboard was built as part of the prime app wave 1 (TASK-47, TASK-48, TASK-49) but the aggregation writer (TASK-47 scheduled job) was never implemented. The dashboard returns empty/zero data indefinitely without it.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Owner dashboard shows non-zero, real guest KPI data after the aggregation writer runs for the first time. The `ownerKpis/{date}` nodes in Firebase RTDB are populated for recent dates.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Dashboard UI complete. Writer is server-side only.
Dashboard shows empty-state copy ("No guest data available") gracefully. Writer does not affect UX directly.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: The empty-state message will disappear once the writer runs — this is the intended outcome.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: RTDB rules restrict `ownerKpis` write to `messagingUsers/{uid}/role === 'owner OR admin OR developer'`. `firebase-custom-token.ts` already exists for generating service-account custom tokens. `staff-auth-session.ts` shows the pattern for custom-token-based Firebase auth.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: `CF_FIREBASE_API_KEY` alone is unlikely to satisfy role check (it is an Identity Toolkit key, not a Firebase Auth UID with a role entry). Writer needs either: (a) a dedicated `messagingUsers` entry with `developer` role for a service account UID, exchanged via custom token flow; or (b) a relaxed RTDB rule for server-to-server writes to `ownerKpis`.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — resolve service-account auth path before implementation

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No existing logging in `kpiAggregator.ts` (pure function). `kpiReader.ts` logs errors to `console.error`.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Writer should log: date processed, guestCount aggregated, success/failure, and any Firebase write errors. No alerting hook exists for aggregation failures.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — add structured logging to writer

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Aggregation logic fully tested. Read layer fully tested. No writer tests exist.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Writer test needed. Integration test for Firebase query shape needed.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `DailyKpiRecord` schema and write path `ownerKpis/{date}` defined. `RawDayData` input interface exists but does not match actual RTDB schema (confirmed by factcheck). Actual signals distributed across 6 RTDB roots.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: `kpiAggregator.ts`'s `RawDayData` input interface needs redesign or a projection shim so the writer can feed it from actual RTDB paths. `checkInDate` is NOT indexed on `bookings` (confirmed: `database.rules.json` indexes `start_time`, `occupants` only).
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — redesign aggregator input OR build projection shim; confirm date-lookup strategy

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Budget baseline: 7 reads per 7-day view. Write cost: 1 write per date per aggregation run (idempotent upsert).
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: `bookings` node has no `checkInDate` index → full scan required. Each aggregation run for one date requires: 1 bookings scan + N reads across 5 additional roots (one per guest per root) = O(N * 5) reads. For 10 guests on a date that is ~50 reads. Acceptable for a daily job, but must be designed as a batch with bounded parallelism. Writer must be idempotent (re-running same date overwrites cleanly).
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — design bounded-parallelism batch; implement idempotent upsert

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No feature flag on the writer (it does not exist yet). `canAccessStaffOwnerRoutes` gates dashboard reads in production.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: First run will populate RTDB with real data — no rollback path for that data. Writer endpoint should be protected (staff/admin only). Rollback = disable the trigger/cron; existing zero-default behavior is the fallback state.

open_operator_questions: Should the writer be triggered via (A) a new CF Pages Function endpoint, (B) a GitHub Actions cron job, or (C) a separate Cloudflare Worker?
What Firebase auth credential should the writer use to satisfy the RTDB `ownerKpis` write rule?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The owner dashboard (`/owner` and `/owner/scorecard`) exists and is fully wired to a read layer (`kpiReader.ts`) that pulls pre-aggregated daily KPI nodes from Firebase RTDB at `ownerKpis/{date}`. However, **nothing ever writes to those nodes**. The aggregation logic (`kpiAggregator.ts`) is a collection of pure functions with no caller in the codebase. The TASK-47 "scheduled job" referenced in the lineage doc does not exist. Until that writer is built and scheduled, the dashboard will silently return zeros for every metric on every day. **Critical finding from factcheck:** The `kpiAggregator.t
goals: Build the missing aggregation writer that calls `aggregateDailyKpis()` and persists results to `ownerKpis/{date}` in Firebase RTDB.
Provide a trigger mechanism (Cloudflare Pages cron, or on-demand API endpoint, or manual backfill command) so the job can run.
Validate that after the first run the dashboard shows real data rather than an empty-state placeholder.
non_goals: Changing the dashboard UI (`owner/page.tsx`, `owner/scorecard/page.tsx`) — already complete.
Changing the read layer (`kpiReader.ts`) — already complete and tested.
Changing the aggregation logic (`kpiAggregator.ts`) — already complete and unit-tested.
Adding new KPI metrics beyond those already defined in `DailyKpiRecord`.
constraints: The prime app deploys as Cloudflare Pages + Functions. Cloudflare Pages does not support cron triggers natively — cron is only available in Workers.

The app uses Firebase REST client (`FirebaseRest` class in `functions/lib/firebase-rest`) for server-side Firebase writes in existing CF Pages functions. The aggregation writer should follow the same pattern.

Firebase RTDB rules at `ownerKpis` restrict writes to authenticated users with `owner`, `admin`, or `developer` role (`messagingUsers/{uid}/role`). The writer must use an authenticated credential with one of these roles. The existing `CF_FIREBASE_API_KEY` is an Identity Toolkit key; `firebase-rest.ts` passes it as an RTDB `auth=` token, which is an informal pattern. A proper service-account credential using `firebase-custom-token.ts` (already exists) is the correct production path.

The `aggregateDailyKpis()` pure function's `RawDayData` interface (`bookings/{bookingId}/occupants/{uuid}/preArrival` etc.) does NOT match the actual RTDB schema. The actual data lives across multiple top-level roots (see Critical Finding in Summary). The writer needs a projection layer or the aggregator interface needs redesign.
The `bookings` node is indexed on `start_time` and `occupants`, not `checkInDate`. `checkInDate` is definitively not indexed — querying by it would require a full scan of all bookings.
key_entry_points: `apps/prime/src/app/owner/page.tsx` — SSR async page that calls `readKpiRange()` directly. Displays 4 KPI cards + daily breakdown. Protected by `canAccessStaffOwnerRoutes()`.

`apps/prime/src/app/owner/scorecard/page.tsx` — SSR async page that calls `readKpiRange()` + `computeBusinessScorecard()`. Shows 5 metric categories with targets + operating review. Protected by same gate.
`apps/prime/src/app/owner/setup/page.tsx` — Setup page; shows `ActivationFunnelSummary` component (reads `localStorage`, client-only). Not part of the pipeline gap.

[Missing Domain Coverage] [Advisory]: Write rule requires role check — auth strategy for server-side write is an open question

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Missing Domain Coverage] [Advisory]: No cron trigger in prime app. Options identified (CF Worker, GitHub Actions, on-demand endpoint) but decision deferred to operator.

[System Boundary Coverage] [Blocking]: `checkInDate` is definitively NOT indexed in `database.rules.json` (only `start_time`, `occupants`). Full scan confirmed as necessary unless `roomsByDate/{date}` is usable.

[Missing Domain Coverage] [Blocking]: `kpiAggregator.ts` `RawDayData` assumes `bookings/{bookingId}/occupants/{uuid}/preArrival` etc. Actual schema distributes signals across 6 roots: `preArrival/`, `checkInCodes/byUuid/`, `checkins/{date}/`, `primeRequests/byGuest/`, `bagStorage/`. Writer must project from real paths.
None — writer tests documented as gap; existing patterns provide clear seam for new tests
[System Boundary Coverage] [Advisory]: `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_STAFF_OWNER_ROUTES` env var status in production unknown

process_topology.trigger: None — no current process exists to write `ownerKpis/{date}` nodes.

areas.label: KPI read path
areas.flow: `OwnerPage` and `ScorecardPage` call `readKpiRange(start, end)` → `readDailyKpi(date)` → Firebase `get(ownerKpis/{date})` → returns `ZERO_SAFE_DEFAULTS` if node missing
areas.issues: Silently returns zeros; no error raised. Dashboard shows "No guest data available for this period."

areas.label: KPI write path
areas.flow: **Does not exist.** `aggregateDailyKpis()` is a pure function with no caller outside tests.
areas.issues: No writer, no scheduler, no trigger. `ownerKpis/` subtree in RTDB is permanently empty.

areas.label: RTDB data topology
areas.flow: The actual signal data lives across 6 RTDB roots: `bookings/{bookingId}/{uuid}` (booking/occupant records), `preArrival/{uuid}` (checklist + ETA), `checkInCodes/byUuid/{uuid}` (codes), `checkins/{date}/{uuid}` (actual checkins), `primeRequests/byGuest/{uuid}` (extension/bag-drop), `bagStorage/{uuid}` (bag drops)
areas.issues: `kpiAggregator.ts`'s `RawDayData` interface assumes all data under `bookings/{id}/occupants` — this does not match the live schema. The writer must perform a multi-path join.

areas.label: Access gate
areas.flow: `canAccessStaffOwnerRoutes()` returns `true` in dev; in production requires `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_STAFF_OWNER_ROUTES === 'true'`
areas.issues: Production access status unknown — env var not confirmed as set in CF Pages deployment.

areas.label: Firebase auth for write
areas.flow: `ownerKpis` write rule requires `messagingUsers/{uid}/role === 'owner' OR 'admin' OR 'developer'`. The app has a `firebase-custom-token.ts` library for generating service-account-based custom tokens for auth.
areas.owners: CF Pages secrets → Firebase Auth → RTDB
areas.issues: Current `FirebaseRest` read/write pattern uses `CF_FIREBASE_API_KEY` as an RTDB `auth=` token — this works for unprotected paths but is unlikely to satisfy the `messagingUsers` role check for `ownerKpis`. Proper auth requires a custom token or relaxing the RTDB rule for server-side writes.
